docs.title: Translation Status
docs.text:
This table tracks translation progress for the MkDocs site. English (docs/en/) is the source of truth. Nepali (docs/ne/) pages are created as stubs and translated progressively.

Page English Nepali Notes Home ✅ ✅ Home page stub translated Getting Started / Project Overview ✅ ✅ (stub) Needs full translation Guides / Contributors ✅ ✅ (stubs) submitting-data, using-forms, understanding-review stubs present Guides / Developers ✅ ⬜ Backend & auth pages will be prioritized later API / Reference ✅ ⬜ Auto-generated OpenAPI snapshot; examples may need translation

Maintainers: open PRs with docs/ne/ changes to add Nepali translations. Use this file to update status when pages are translated.

docs.title: Roadmap & Platform Plan
docs.text:
This page summarizes the phased roadmap for HeritageGraph (from PLATFORM_PLAN.md).

Phases:

  • Phase 1: Assertion-first architecture, multi-step forms, contributor onboarding
  • Phase 2: Record pages, triaged reviewer workspace, improved UX
  • Phase 3: Syncretic relationships, calendar conversions, mobile field surveys

For detailed milestones and implementation notes see PLATFORM_PLAN.md in the repository.

docs.title: AI Agents Guide (migration from AGENTS.md)
docs.text: This page provides a condensed orientation for AI agents and integrators working with HeritageGraph.

docs.title: What HeritageGraph is
docs.text:
• Full-stack platform to preserve and publish cultural heritage as linked open data.
  • Backend: Django REST Framework. Frontend: Next.js 15 dashboard, Next.js 14 landing.
  • Auth: NextAuth v4 with Google OAuth in production; Credentials/SimpleJWT in development.

docs.title: Key conventions for agents
docs.text:
• Read AGENTS.md in the repository root for the full agent-oriented instructions.
  • Avoid committing secrets; use environment variables from .env.example.
  • New features should use the CulturalEntity → Revision → Activity workflow not the legacy Submission.

docs.title: Useful endpoints (summary)
docs.text:
• Health: /health/, /health/detailed/, /health/ready/, /health/live/
  • API docs: /docs, /redoc/, /schema/
  • Heritage data: /data/submissions/, /data/cultural-entities/, /data/review/
  • CIDOC data: /cidoc/persons/, /cidoc/events/, etc.

docs.title: Where to look in the codebase
docs.text:
• Backend apps live in heritage_graph/apps/ (heritage_data, cidoc_data).
  • Frontend app code lives under heritage_graph_ui/src/app/.
  • Important files: AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, FORMS.md, AUTH.md.

This guide will be expanded and linked into the main MkDocs site. For the canonical agent instructions, read AGENTS.md in the repository root.

docs.title: Production Deployment (summary)
docs.text:
This page summarizes the production deployment steps. For full details see DEPLOYMENT.md in the repository root.

Key points: - Traefik for TLS and routing - PostgreSQL as the DB - Use environment variables for secrets; do not commit .env - Health endpoints: /health/, /health/ready/, /health/live/

docs.title: Architecture — System Design & Data Flow
docs.text: This is a condensed migration of ARCHITECTURE.md summarizing system topology, data models, authentication flow, and frontend architecture.

docs.title: System components
docs.text:
• Traefik: reverse proxy and TLS termination
  • Backend: Django REST Framework (port 8000)
  • Frontend: Next.js 15 dashboard (port 3000)
  • Landing: Next.js 14 marketing site (port 3000)
  • PostgreSQL: primary data store

docs.title: Network & routing
docs.text:
• Docker networks: proxy (traefik), backend (internal)
  • Hostnames in development use *.localhost (frontend.localhost, backend.localhost)

docs.title: Authentication flow (summary)
docs.text:
• Production: NextAuth Google flow → browser receives Google ID token → API calls include Authorization: Bearer <id_token> → Django verifies token and auto-creates users.
  • Development: Credentials flow → POST /api/token/ returns SimpleJWT tokens → use Authorization: Bearer <access>.

docs.title: Data models
docs.text:
• Legacy: Submission model (flat fields) — used by older forms.
  • New: CulturalEntity → Revision (JSONField) → Activity workflow — preferred for new features.
  • CIDOC-CRM structured models in cidoc_data for person/location/event domains.

docs.title: Frontend app structure
docs.text:
• src/app/dashboard/ contains knowledge, contribute, curation, community, and graphview sections.
  • Use useSession() for client auth and getServerSession() on server components.

For the full original file with diagrams and detailed flow charts, see ARCHITECTURE.md in the repository root. This page is meant for quick orientation; deeper architectural decisions and diagrams remain in the canonical file until fully migrated.

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
HeritageGraph preserves and publishes cultural heritage as linked open data. This page gives a short overview for newcomers.

Key goals: - Enable community contributions via web forms - Provide a moderation & epistemic review workflow - Publish data as linked open data aligned to CIDOC-CRM

See ARCHITECTURE.md, PLATFORM_PLAN.md, and AGENTS.md in the repository for the full vision and roadmap.

docs.title: Contributors Guide
docs.text:
Welcome contributors — this section explains how to submit cultural heritage data, use contribution forms, and what happens during review.

Planned pages:

  • Submitting data: submitting-data.md (walkthrough)
  • Using forms: using-forms.md (field-level guidance)
  • Understanding review: understanding-review.md (what reviewers do)

For now, see the canonical FORMS.md and ONTOLOGY.md in the repository for detailed field and registry information. We will migrate and combine those into guides/developers/forms-system.md.

docs.title: Authentication Guide (Developers)
docs.text: This page summarizes how authentication works in HeritageGraph and provides quick patterns for frontend and backend developers.

docs.title: Two modes: Development vs Production
docs.text:
• Development: DevSessionAuthentication + SimpleJWT. Frontend uses a CredentialsProvider and POST /api/token/ to obtain {access, refresh}.
  • Production: GoogleTokenAuthentication. Frontend uses NextAuth with the Google provider; API calls send Authorization: Bearer <google_id_token>.

Detection is automatic: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID env var present → production mode.

docs.title: Frontend patterns
docs.text:
• Client components: use useSession() to get session.accessToken and call backend with Authorization: Bearer ${session.accessToken}.
  • Server components / API routes: use getServerSession(authOptions) and forward the accessToken to the backend when making server-side requests.

Example client fetch:

const res = await fetch(`${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL}/data/submissions/`, {
  headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${session.accessToken}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
});

docs.title: Backend verification
docs.text:
• In production, GoogleTokenAuthentication validates the Google ID token with google-auth and auto-creates/syncs a Django User + UserProfile.
  • In development, JWTs are verified via SimpleJWT.

docs.title: Quick checks
docs.text:
• Use INTERNAL_BACKEND_URL for server-side requests in Docker (http://backend:8000).
  • Ensure NEXTAUTH_SECRET is set for NextAuth.

For full reference, see the repository AUTH.md and the heritage_graph/apps/heritage_data/authentication.py implementation.

docs.title: Contributing (Developer Guide)
docs.text:
This guide replaces the minimal contributing.md at the repo root and describes how to contribute code.

Essentials:

  • Branch from v1 for all work.
  • Commit messages: imperative tense (e.g., Add feature).
  • Open issues for non-trivial changes and assign a clear description and acceptance criteria.
  • Use ruff format . and ruff check . before submitting PRs.

PR checklist:

  • [ ] Branch from v1
  • [ ] Include tests for backend changes
  • [ ] Run ruff format and npm run build for frontend changes
  • [ ] Update documentation in docs/ for any public API changes

See also CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md for developer conventions and AI-agent guidance.

docs.title: Coding Conventions (Backend & Frontend)
docs.text: This page condenses the most important guidelines from CLAUDE.md and CONVENTIONS.md for contributors.

docs.title: Backend (Django)
docs.text:
• Python version: 3.13. Use ruff for formatting and linting.
  • New models: use UUID primary keys, created_at / updated_at, and explicit db_table.
  • Prefer CulturalEntity/Revision/Activity for new data models.
  • Use ModelViewSet and DRF @action for custom endpoints.

docs.title: Frontend (Next.js + TypeScript)
docs.text:
• Next.js 15, React 19. Use TypeScript for all new components.
  • Use named exports and props interface for components.
  • Use Tailwind CSS utilities; manage colors in globals.css via tweakcn.
  • Use use client only when necessary.

docs.title: API and Docs
docs.text:
• Use @extend_schema() to document critical endpoints for drf-spectacular.
  • Keep API examples and embed an OpenAPI snapshot under docs/static/openapi.json.

docs.title: Git & PRs
docs.text:
• Branch from v1. Use imperative commit messages. Include tests for backend changes.

This page will be expanded with examples and links to the full CLAUDE.md content.

docs.title: Forms System & Ontology (summary)
docs.text:
This page will merge the existing FORMS.md and ONTOLOGY.md into a single authoritative guide about the registry-driven form system, field types, enums, and migration patterns.

Current status: the canonical files FORMS.md and ONTOLOGY.md remain in the repository root. This page will be completed by consolidating those sources and removing duplication.

Key concepts to include: - Field registry and types - How to add a new entity type - Mapping LinkML/schema to frontend registry - CIDOC-CRM alignment and provenance via PROV-O

docs.title: Skills Matrix
docs.text: This page will mirror SKILLS.md and track feature status across the platform (working/partial/planned). For now consult SKILLS.md in the repository for the authoritative table.

docs.title: Translation & i18n Workflow
docs.text:
We use mkdocs-static-i18n to provide English (en) and Nepali (ne) content.

Workflow:

  1. English is the source of truth; write new content under docs/en/.
  2. Create corresponding Nepali stubs under docs/ne/ with [Translation needed] markers.
  3. Translators update docs/ne/* pages; pull requests should include both en and ne changes where available.
  4. CI builds the MkDocs site and publishes to GitHub Pages. The language switcher appears in the site header.

Priority pages for translation:

  • index.md (home)
  • guides/contributors/*
  • getting-started/project-overview.md
  • about/roadmap.md

Translation status can be tracked in docs/translation-status.md (TODO: add table).

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text:
This page is a mirror of TROUBLESHOOTING.md in the repository root. It will be expanded with actionable debugging steps and links to logs.

Common issues: - Duplicate NextAuth config - Hardcoded backend URLs in README - Docker compose quirks — use make setup to reproduce the recommended workflow

See TROUBLESHOOTING.md for the full list of known issues and fixes.

docs.title: रोडम्याप (नेपाली)
docs.text:
This page is a Nepali placeholder copied from the English roadmap; please translate when ready.

Roadmap and major phases (Phase 1, Phase 2, Phase 3) will be summarized here.

docs.title: परियोजना अवलोकन (नेपाली)
docs.text:
This is a Nepali placeholder containing the English project overview for translation.

# Project Overview

HeritageGraph preserves and publishes cultural heritage as linked open data. This page gives a short overview for newcomers.

Key goals:
- Enable community contributions via web forms
- Provide a moderation & epistemic review workflow
- Publish data as linked open data aligned to CIDOC-CRM

See `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `PLATFORM_PLAN.md`, and `AGENTS.md` in the repository for the full vision and roadmap.

docs.title: परियोजना अवलोकन (नेपाली)
docs.text: [Translation needed] HeritageGraph समुदायमा आधारित सांस्कृतिक सम्पदा डाटा सङ्कलन र प्रकाशन गर्ने प्लेटफर्म हो। यो पृष्ठ छोटो परिचय र लक्ष्य बताउनेछ।

docs.title: योगदानकर्ताहरूका लागि मार्गदर्शन (नेपाली)
docs.text:
यस सेक्सनले योगदानकर्ताहरूले कसरी सांस्कृतिक सम्पदा दर्ता गर्ने, फर्महरू कसरी प्रयोग गर्ने र समीक्षा प्रक्रियामा के हुन्छ भन्ने छोटो मार्गदर्शन समावेश गर्दछ।

यो पृष्ठ अझै विकासको अवस्थामा छ — पूर्ण सामग्री अंग्रेजीमा docs/en/guides/contributors/ मा छ र क्रमशः अनुवाद गरिनेछ।

docs.title: HeritageGraph Documentation (नेपाली)
docs.text:
Welcome — this Nepali section is under active translation. For full English docs see the main site.

Original English content:

# HeritageGraph Documentation

Welcome to the HeritageGraph documentation site. This site collects guides and references for contributors, reviewers, developers, institutional partners, API users, and AI integrations.

- Start with Getting Started → Overview
- Developers: see Guides → Developers
- API users: see API → Reference

If you don't find something, please open an issue in the repository.

docs.title: HeritageGraph документація (नेपाली)
docs.text:
यो साइटले योगदानकर्ता, विकासकर्ता र सहभागीहरूको लागि आधारभूत दस्तावेजहरू समेट्नेछ।

यो अहिले प्रगतिको अवस्थामा छ — धेरै पृष्ठहरू अंग्रेजीमा उपलब्ध छन्।

docs.title: Understanding Review (नेपाली placeholder)
docs.text:
This page explains the review workflow, reviewer roles, and what contributors can expect after submission.

Review queues, triage, review decisions (community_reviewer, domain_expert, expert_curator), and timelines.

docs.title: समीक्षा प्रक्रियालाई बुझ्नुहोस् (नेपाली)
docs.text: [Translation needed] यो पृष्ठले समीक्षा प्रक्रियाको तीन-पर्सोना प्रणाली (समुदाय समीक्षक, क्षेत्र विशेषज्ञ, विशेषज्ञ क्यूरेटर) र निर्णयहरू के अर्थ राख्छन् भनेर स्पष्ट पार्नेछ।

docs.text:

Field descriptions, required fields, relation pickers, date formats, coordinate inputs.
